content.problemStatement:
Write a program to calculate the sum of the digits present in an alphanumeric string.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer, T, denoting the number of test cases.
Each of the next T lines contains a string consisting of lowercase/uppercase English letters, along with digits 0 to 9. (There shall be no space in the string)


Output Format

For each test case, print the sum of digits in each string.
constraints: Strings contain only printable ASCII characters; no spaces.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single string consisting of lowercase/uppercase English letters, along with digits 0 to 9.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the sum of digits in each string.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Sum of digits 1 through 9 plus 0 equals 45.

solution.approach: The key insight is to iterate over each character in the string and check if it's a digit. If it's a digit, add its value to the sum. This approach works because the problem only asks for the sum of digits in the string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum length of a string. This is because we're iterating over each character in each string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) because we're only using a constant amount of space to store the sum and the current character.
